Who's got a money one they think is particularly interesting? Or I'll just pick people. Who's got a money one? You're own. Nice succinct philosophy, Darren. So you're a relativity guy, not an object guy. Yeah, okay. Anybody differ? Dramatically? Yeah. Do you mean my answer or...? Yeah, I mean, you would differ. Yeah. I wrote down it's not... Yeah, there's room for both by the way. I mean, it's a very interesting long-term debate, by the way. It's great cocktail stuff late at night. I'm on her side, by the way. See, I think there are fundamental black and whites in most categories. So, like, if your kid is breaking into houses and robbing houses and setting fire to cats, see, I don't think you're a successful parent, period. I don't think you get the right to call yourself a successful parent. I don't think...I think society needs to say you are not a successful parent. So I'm an objective...a black and white objective standards guy. But I understand a relative...you know, I understand the give them room to make their own definition argument. That being said, I would still...I would still encourage them to consider success being losing 80 pounds and learning their cholesterol. I would come out and say this will stack your...stack the deck in your favor. Can I make...I'll make one sort of observation and suggestion. That the greatest influence comes from certainty, from concreteness, from black and white positions. And the more gentle you are, the more room you allow for people to create their own definitions of whatever it is you are attempting to influence them about, which in your case is health and fitness more than it is anything else. Actually, the less influence you have. I mean, so people look for concrete positions. And to the extent that of course, you know, they oversimplify what they should be looking for and we oversimplify what we communicate. But nonetheless... Yeah, look, there's a certain segment of course who, you know, prefer...you know, I'll support you even if I disagree with you about certain things. We can't be in disagreement about everything. But I'd rather know where you stand. You know, there is that. But beyond that, there's the constant separation of, you know, you got to be willing to dump, you know, to repel and send away a large number of people in order to have enormous impact on a small number of people. And the way you have enormous impact on a small number of people is by being very, very definite about your philosophical positions. You know, you're okay. Give me a time. Who's got a time one? Philosophical position on time. Discipline allows me to make the best use of my time and get the greatest enjoyment from my time. So to make that a philosophical position now, you value discipline above many other things, if not all things. I think discipline is the way to get the benefit that I want from my time through the discipline, by being disciplined to the schedule. So to go to our John and Herne debate, John and Chris debate, you would define a disciplined person as successful or more correctly, you define a very undisciplined person as unsuccessful. You would call it a make or break. No, I'd say that being disciplined gives you a greater chance to be successful. And that concreteness, that's what people want it. You know? So like Saturday night people are going to ask me what horse to bet on. See, there's no good answer to that question. There's a real complicated answer to that question. Nobody wants a complicated answer. Right? They just want to tell me what horse to bet on. Right? And so to whatever degree, I'm not suggesting you sacrifice your personal beliefs or your integrity for money. I'm not suggesting it. Sometimes it's necessary, but I'm not suggesting it. But what I am saying to you is that the less wiggle room you give them, the firmer you are. Right? See, the reason the three-step letter campaign worked so well for me is because I was willing to set aside the fact that it shouldn't always be a three-step letter campaign. Right? And I'll tell you that later. But at point of connection with you, here, it's three steps. They're 15 days apart. Each one refers to the previous one. That's it. Right? Well, people got that. Right?
